duplicates.title_a: SEBI Master Circular for Mutual Funds
duplicates.title_b: SEBI Master Circular for Mutual Funds

reference.title: SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 2026
reference.context: Permits mutual funds to borrow funds for specific purposes and sets limits.

reference.title: SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 2026
reference.context: Exempts intraday borrowings from the 20% limit subject to conditions.

reference.title: SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 2026
reference.context: AMCs shall ensure compliance of clauses 6 and 7 of Fourth Schedule of SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 2026 and para 16.8 of SEBI Master Circular for Mutual Funds dated June 27, 2024.

reference.title: SEBI Master Circular for Mutual Funds
reference.context: AMCs shall ensure compliance of clauses 6 and 7 of Fourth Schedule of SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 2026 and para 16.8 of SEBI Master Circular for Mutual Funds dated June 27, 2024.

reference.title: SEBI Master Circular for Mutual Funds
reference.context: In line with para 10.9 of SEBI Master Circular for Mutual Funds dated June 27, 2024, cost of intraday borrowing, if any, shall be borne by the AMC.

verdict.reason: The circular_number should be empty as it is not a specific circular number but a date.

reference.title: SEBI Circular
reference.context: SEBI has introduced Closing Auction Session in the equity cash segment of the Stock Exchanges vide circular reference no. HO/47/11/11(3)2025-MRD-POD2/I/2765/2026 dated January 16, 2026, effective from August 03, 2026.

reference.title: Securities and Exchange Board of India Act, 1992
reference.context: This circular is issued in exercise of powers conferred under Section 11 (1) of the Securities and Exchange Board of India Act, 1992, read with the provisions of Regulation 77 of SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 1996, to protect the interest of investors in securities and to promote the development of, and to regulate the securities market.

reference.title: SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 1996
reference.context: This circular is issued in exercise of powers conferred under Section 11 (1) of the Securities and Exchange Board of India Act, 1992, read with the provisions of Regulation 77 of SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 1996, to protect the interest of investors in securities and to promote the development of, and to regulate the securities market.
